evals.prompt: Review this landing page UI before launch and tell me what feels off: src/app/page.tsx
evals.expected_output: Keeps the task in a broad UI audit lane, fetches the current Vercel guidelines, and reports concise location-based findings.
assertions: Response keeps the task in web-design-guidelines rather than immediately routing it away
Response fetches or explicitly plans to fetch the latest Vercel Web Interface Guidelines command
Response uses concise location-based findings or an equivalent concrete review format

evals.prompt: Audit this modal for keyboard traps, screen-reader labels, and focus return behavior.
evals.expected_output: Routes the task to web-accessibility instead of keeping broad UI review as the main lane.
assertions: Response routes the task to web-accessibility
Response does not present web-design-guidelines as the primary skill for WCAG-focused remediation
Response preserves the distinction between broad UI review and accessibility-first implementation work

evals.prompt: Open my logged-in dashboard in the browser and tell me which states are visually broken after I click around.
evals.expected_output: Routes runtime browser validation to playwriter instead of pretending a static code review is sufficient.
assertions: Response routes the browser-validation phase to playwriter
Response does not claim that static code review alone is enough for this runtime request
Response preserves web-design-guidelines as a code or audit surface rather than a browser automation surface
